(ii)        it must be completed in accordance with the relevant provisions of paragraph 4; or


(b)        orally, the applicant must—


(i)         explain to the court why notice has not been given; and


(ii)        detail the information required by paragraph 4.3 to the court.


6.6       Where the application is made on notice it must be served in accordance with paragraph 10.


6.7       This paragraph applies to an application for any variation or discharge of a DAPO in existing proceedings as it applies to an application for a DAPO in existing proceedings, except such an application, if in writing, is to be made using Form DA3.


## III — PARTIES


## 7. Parties


7.1 Where the person to be protected is not the applicant (whether or not that person is a child aged 16 or 17) the court must consider, at every stage in the proceedings, whether to join that person as a party to the proceedings.


7.2       The court may join the person to be protected as a party to proceedings if it considers it is in the best interests of that person to do so.


7.3       Where the court joins a child as a party to proceedings, the court must consider if the child should have a litigation friend to conduct the proceedings on the child’s behalf.


7.4       A person, including the person to be protected, may make an application to the court for that person or another person to—


(a)        be joined as a party to the proceedings; or


(b)        cease to be a party to the proceedings,


under Part 18.


7.5       As soon as practicable after receiving an application under paragraph 7.4, the court must do one of following—


(a)        grant the application, where it is under paragraph 7.4(a);


(b)        order that the application be considered at a hearing and fix a date; or


(c)        invite written representations as to whether the application should be granted, to be filed within a specified period, and on expiry of that period act as it sees fit under sub-paragraph (a) or (b).


7.6       The court officer must inform—


(a)        the applicant under paragraph 7.4;
